In the second place, but far behind, was the Dominican Republic. A global tourism powerhouse By developing airport infrastructure and calibrating new direct flights, Mexico has set international tourism high on its priorities list. At the forefront of Mexico's spike in international tourism is the FONATUR, or Mexico's National Fund for Tourism Development. The tourism body worked in the expansion of Cancun's airport and other four key tourist airports in the country. Plus, Mexico City's airport added new international direct flights in the 2010s, resulting in being rated as the best internationally connected airport in Latin America in 2019. With this and other developments in this sector, the North American country managed to rank among the top 20 destinations in the Travel and Tourism Competitiveness Ranking that year. South America is filled with opportunities for adventure. There are so many things to see and do, it may be difficult to decide on a particular destination. Because of the disruption to international tourism caused by COVID-19, we’re using data from 2019, the last full year prior to the outbreak, provided by The World Bank database. As with other regions in the world, numbers are steadily climbing nearer to pre-Covid levels in South America. South America’s diversity means it can feel like you’ve traveled the entire world by exploring just this one continent. Mexico continues to dwarf South America for the sheer number of tourist arrivals, with Mexico City the most-visited capital in Latin America (the Dominican Republic is the second most-visited country). However, South American travel is slowly closing the gap. 1. Argentina The top destination for international tourists in South America continues to be Argentina. Brazil Brazil offers tourists a wide range of leisure options to choose from within the range of the many activities available in the country. Nature provides the tourist with a combination of leisure, recreation, and ecotourism. The sun and beaches, especially in Copacabana in Rio de Janeiro, attract especially the sunbathers. The lush green Amazon Rainforest, Iguacu Falls, and Carnival offer a perfect site for nature lovers. The numerous magnificent buildings with gorgeous architectural designs such as Edificio Copan, Panoramic Tower, and colonial buildings have also served as tourist attraction sites in the country. In 2015 the country attracted 6.31 million international tourists mostly from countries such as Argentina, the United States, Chile, Paraguay, France, and Germany. South America remains the primary source of foreign tourists visiting the country. Argentina Argentina’s vast and varied climates and micro-climates, and the presence of such natural wonders as Aconcagua Mountain (the highest point outside the Himalayas), the River Plata (the widest river in the world), the Iguazu Falls, and the Argentine meadow plains are just some of the places that have attracted tourists into the country for years. Travel to South America - Most Visited Countries - Amazon Adventures Brazil, with icons such as Rio, the Amazon and Iguassu Falls topped the chart. But surprisingly, Peru, with mighty Machu Picchu, came in 4th, barely beating ... To my even bigger surprise, Ecuadorand the Galapagos Islandslagged behind those countries and had just slightly higher numbers than Paraguay. But Ecuador has so much more to offer (in my opinion)! Where else can you find so much variety in such a small area and with such friendly people? What could be behind these strange (to me) numbers? I think that one factor is cruises. There are now many large ships cruising between Chile, Uruguay and Argentina and others that are stopping in Cartagena, Colombia. Many people flock to the coast of Uruguay (Punta del Este, etc.)during high season many people cross the border from Iguassu Falls into Cuided del Este Paraguay just to shop for few hours (I asked our operator at the falls and they thought that was the main reason) I can understand Chile being up there. It is safe and has been getting a lot of publicity as an adventure travel destination as well as for it's variety of areas - desert, beach, mountains/volcanoes and lakes. And this year we have been selling lots of self-drive packages to there. But more people go to Paraguay than to Bolivia? Lake Titicaca and Uyuni are very popular destinations for us and it is a great destination for people who want authentic culture. We've tried offering some interesting sites in Paraguay, but all of the tour operators seem to be only interested in promoting Asuncion, Ciudad del Este and the missions.
